title: Deadlift 1RM Calculator (Epley) | Hacé Cuentas
description: Estimate your deadlift one-rep max using the Epley formula. Free, fast, and accurate.
intro: Athletic training is built on physiological principles: adaptation, supercompensation, and specificity. Training zones, weekly load, and recovery are cornerstones of performance. Estimate your deadlift 1RM using the Epley formula—free and fast. Our data is current for 2026 and reviewed regularly.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **1RM = weight × (1 + reps/30)**. Apply directly with your input values. Deadlift 1RM by Epley.
useCases: Validate theoretical results before applying them
Teaching and learning about one-rep max strength

fields.label: Weight (kg)
fields.help: Weight of the load lifted (bar and plates) in kg.

explanation:
**Athletic training** combines science (physiology, biomechanics) and practice. Training intensity zones, progressive overload, rest, and nutrition form a complete system.

## How It's Calculated

The Epley formula estimates your deadlift 1RM. It's quick and free.

This calculator applies the formula with your inputs, checking typical ranges and alerting you to unreasonable values.

## Important Notes

This calculation is a reference only. For critical decisions (medical, financial, legal), consult a qualified professional. Our data is current for 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.a: 1RM = weight × (1 + reps/30). The formula is most accurate for 1–10 reps; beyond 10 reps it tends to underestimate.

faq.q: How accurate is the Epley estimate?
faq.a: Very accurate for 1–10 reps. Beyond 10 reps, accuracy decreases. Always test conservatively and prioritize safety.

faq.q: Should I test my 1RM directly or use an estimator?
faq.a: An estimator is safer and works well for program planning. A direct 1RM test gives the exact number but carries higher injury risk—only do this with a coach or spotter and proper warm-up.

faq.q: Can I use this for other lifts (squat, bench press)?
faq.a: Yes. The Epley formula works for any barbell lift. Use the weight you lifted and the reps completed.

faq.q: How do I improve my deadlift?
faq.a: Use progressive overload, moderate volume (6–15 reps), adequate recovery, and proper form. Linear progression works well for beginners.

faq.q: What's a good warm-up before testing 1RM?
faq.a: Use an ascending pyramid: empty bar, then gradually add weight with short rest (1–2 min) between sets. Don't fatigue yourself before the attempt.
Yes. All Hacé Cuentas calculators are free, no registration needed, and work entirely in your browser.

faq.q: Is my data saved or shared?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. We don't send any data to our servers or third parties.

faq.q: What if my result seems wrong?
faq.a: Check your inputs (weight and reps). If using reps beyond 10, the estimate may be conservative. When in doubt, train conservatively and test with a coach.
